description: Life-threatening emergency — acute coronary occlusion
default_action: Immediate PCI referral — door-to-balloon <90 min

description: Cardiac arrest — pulseless
default_action: Start ACLS — immediate defibrillation

description: Life-threatening ventricular arrhythmia
default_action: Assess pulse — cardioversion if pulseless or haemodynamically unstable

description: Complete AV dissociation — haemodynamic emergency
default_action: Emergent temporary pacing — cardiology stat

description: High-risk block — unpredictable progression to complete block
default_action: Urgent cardiology review — prepare for pacing

description: High-risk ACS — ongoing myocardial injury
default_action: Immediate anticoagulation and urgent cardiology referral

description: Sudden cardiac death risk — especially at night or with fever
default_action: Urgent electrophysiology referral — ICD consideration

description: Potentially unstable rhythm — stroke and haemodynamic risk
default_action: Rate or rhythm control — CHA2DS2-VASc anticoagulation assessment

description: Haemodynamically significant arrhythmia
default_action: Vagal manoeuvres or adenosine — cardioversion if unstable

description: Rapid ventricular rate — 2:1 block common
default_action: Rate control — cardioversion — anticoagulation consideration

description: Usually benign but monitor for progression — especially if wide QRS
default_action: Cardiology review — ECG monitoring

description: High-risk ACS equivalent — ongoing ischaemia without necrosis
default_action: Aspirin anticoagulation — urgent cardiology referral — serial ECG

description: New LBBB with chest pain is STEMI-equivalent per Sgarbossa criteria
default_action: Urgent cardiology review — rule out ACS — serial ECG and troponin

description: Torsades de Pointes risk — can degenerate to VF
default_action: Stop QT-prolonging drugs — correct K+ Mg2+ — monitor

description: Inflammatory pericardial condition — usually self-limiting
default_action: NSAIDs — rest — cardiology review — rule out myocarditis

description: Chronic structural condition — hypertensive heart disease
default_action: Blood pressure control — outpatient cardiology evaluation

description: LVH with strain pattern — lateral ST-T changes are secondary — STEMI mimic
default_action: Outpatient evaluation — rule out concomitant ischaemia

description: Conduction abnormality — often benign if no prior history
default_action: Outpatient cardiology review — evaluate for underlying cause

description: Usually benign — check for medication effect (digoxin beta-blocker)
default_action: Outpatient review — medication check

description: Slow heart rate — assess symptoms and cause
default_action: Symptom assessment — medication review — evaluate for sick sinus

description: Sinus rate >100 bpm — almost always secondary
default_action: Identify and treat underlying cause — pain fever anaemia dehydration

description: Normal ECG finding
default_action: No action required

description: ECG pattern finding — evaluate for STEMI vs pericarditis vs LVH strain vs Brugada
default_action: Urgent clinical assessment — do not act on ECG pattern alone

description: ECG pattern finding — evaluate for NSTEMI vs ischaemia vs drug effect
default_action: Clinical assessment — serial ECG — troponin

description: ECG pattern finding — evaluate for AF or atrial flutter
default_action: Clinical assessment — rhythm strip

description: ECG pattern finding — evaluate for BBB or ventricular rhythm
default_action: Clinical assessment — full 12-lead review

description: ECG pattern finding — possible pre-excitation or WPW
default_action: Clinical assessment — electrophysiology referral if symptomatic
